DIRECT_SCHOLARLY_CORE.definition: Peer-reviewed paper or chapter, academic dissertation, thesis, or proceedings paper directly analyzing the Cantonese construction or contrast.

REFERENCE_GRAMMAR_CORE.definition: Recognized Cantonese reference grammar explicitly analyzing the relevant construction, profile, or boundary.

PRIMARY_CORPUS_ATTESTATION.definition: Reproducible primary corpus release or checked extraction with an exact row, utterance, context, and retrieval path.

CONTROLLED_JUDGMENT_EVIDENCE.definition: Documented blinded panel, controlled experiment, or adjudicated speaker-judgment evidence with instrument and inclusion provenance.

ATTESTATION_ONLY.definition: Dictionary, lesson, drama, manual, media text, isolated example, or similar source that attests a form but does not directly establish construction-level syntax, semantics, productivity, or boundaries.

LEXICAL_OR_PRONUNCIATION_ONLY.definition: Source supports word identity, reading, orthography, gloss, lexical category, or pronunciation without analyzing the construction.

DISCOVERY_LEAD_ONLY.definition: Bibliographic or search lead whose existence is verified but whose substantive content has not been inspected. No linguistic claim may be imported until the source is recovered and regraded.

RUNTIME_OBSERVATION_ONLY.definition: Parser behavior, generated probes, diagnostics, tests, or implementation observations. These may document collisions but have zero linguistic-evidence weight.
